[0m[38;5;15m⏺ [0mDone, captain. Here's the summary:

  [0m[1mFix: Paste not landing at cursor[0m

  [0m[1mPR[0m: https://github.com/jwalin-shah/voice-engine-swift/pull/9

  [0m[1mWhat was wrong[0m

  [0m[38;5;12mpasteViaAppleScript() [0malways returned [0m[38;5;12mtrue [0m(osascript exits 0 regardless of where the keystroke lands), so the CGEvent fallback was never reached. If VoiceEngine's menubar accessory stole focus during the
  recording/transcription cycle, Cmd+V went to VoiceEngine instead of the target app. Text sat on the clipboard but never arrived at the cursor.
 
  [0m[1mWhat changed (Paster.swift only, 3 changes)[0m

  1. [0m[1mSnapshot [0m[1m[38;5;12mfrontmostApplication[0m at the top of [0m[38;5;12mpaste() [0m— captures the real target app before any focus-shifting ops
  2. [0m[1mActivate the target app [0mbefore sending Cmd+V — [0m[38;5;12mapp.activate() [0mrecovers focus if VoiceEngine stole it; a cheap no-op otherwise
  3. [0m[1mBumped clipboard-commit sleep[0m from 20ms → 50ms to reduce the NSPasteboard race window
 
  [0m[1mVerification[0m

  - [0m[38;5;12mswift build [0m— clean, no warnings from Paster.swift
  - [0m[38;5;12mswift test [0m— passes
  
[0m[38;5;7m✻ Baked for 4m 49s[0m
